nodes.content: North Korea possesses nuclear weapons.
nodes.context_or_evidence: This is a general statement of fact regarding North Korea's military capabilities.

nodes.content: North Korea's active-duty army consists of 1.28 million soldiers, making it the fourth-largest in the world.
nodes.context_or_evidence: This is a statement of fact regarding the size of North Korea's military.

nodes.content: North Korea is bordered by China and Russia to the north and by South Korea to the south.
nodes.context_or_evidence: This describes North Korea's geographical location and bordering countries.

nodes.content: As of 2024, North Korea remained one of the world's most isolated countries and one of Asia's poorest, despite slowly renewing cross-border trade with China.
nodes.context_or_evidence: This is a statement of fact regarding North Korea's economic and political status in 2024.

nodes.content: In 2024, Pyongyang announced it was ending all economic cooperation with South Korea.
nodes.context_or_evidence: This is a statement of fact regarding North Korea's economic policy towards South Korea in 2024.

nodes.content: Pyongyang is the capital and largest city of North Korea, with 2.86 million residents.
nodes.context_or_evidence: This is a demographic fact about North Korea's capital city.

nodes.content: North Korea's GDP is $34.9 billion, ranking it 106th out of 197 countries.
nodes.context_or_evidence: This is an economic fact about North Korea's GDP and its global ranking.

nodes.content: North Korea's GDP per capita is $1,319, ranking it 168th out of 197 countries.
nodes.context_or_evidence: This is an economic fact about North Korea's GDP per capita and its global ranking.

nodes.content: North Korea shares land borders with China, South Korea, and Russia.
nodes.context_or_evidence: This is a geographical fact about North Korea's bordering countries.

nodes.content: The Demilitarized Zone (DMZ), a 155-mile-long, 2.5-mile-wide strip of land, separates North Korea from South Korea.
nodes.context_or_evidence: This describes the DMZ and its dimensions.

nodes.content: North Korea is a totalitarian dictatorship with a comprehensive cult of personality around the Kim family.
nodes.context_or_evidence: This is a general assessment of North Korea's political system.

nodes.content: Amnesty International considers North Korea to have the worst human rights record in the world.
nodes.context_or_evidence: This is based on Amnesty International's assessment of human rights conditions in various countries.

nodes.title: Report of the Commission of Inquiry on Human Rights in the Democratic People's Republic of Korea - Wikipedia

nodes.content: The Report of the Commission of Inquiry on Human Rights in the Democratic People's Republic of Korea is a landmark document resulting from investigations on human rights in North Korea commissioned by the United Nations Human Rights Council.
nodes.context_or_evidence: This describes the origin and significance of the UN Commission of Inquiry report.

nodes.content: In June, the UN opened an office in Seoul, South Korea, to monitor North Korea’s human rights record.
nodes.context_or_evidence: This describes the UN's actions to monitor human rights in North Korea.

nodes.content: Pyongyang threatened “toughest counteractions” to this “grave provocation.” in response to the UN opening an office in Seoul to monitor North Korea’s human rights record.
nodes.context_or_evidence: This describes North Korea's reaction to the UN's monitoring efforts.

nodes.content: The Republic of Korea (South Korea) has historically played an integral role in co-sponsoring Human Rights Council resolutions on the situation in North Korea every year from 2008 to 2018.
nodes.context_or_evidence: This describes South Korea's historical involvement in addressing North Korean human rights issues at the UN.

nodes.content: Civilian toll reported in Tehran due to an unspecified event.
nodes.context_or_evidence: Image depicts people looking through rubble outside a building in Tehran, with the caption indicating someone's daughter is under the rubble.

nodes.content: The World Factbook provides key facts and a structured country profile for North Korea.
nodes.context_or_evidence: The World Factbook's North Korea page offers a structured profile with sections covering background, geography, people and society, environment, government, economy, energy, communications, transportation, military and security, space, and transnational issues.

nodes.content: The World Factbook provides key facts and a structured country profile for North Korea.
nodes.context_or_evidence: The World Factbook's North Korea page offers a structured profile with sections covering background, geography, people and society, environment, government, economy, energy, communications, transportation, military and security, space, and transnational issues.

nodes.content: Juche is described as a "man-centered ideology" where man is the master of everything.
nodes.context_or_evidence: Multiple sources describe Juche as an ideology where the 'popular masses are placed in the center of everything' and 'man is the master of everything and decides everything'.

nodes.content: Juche emphasizes nationalism over internationalism.
nodes.context_or_evidence: Juche's strong dedication to nationalism is contrasted with other communist ideologies.

nodes.content: Kim Jong Il further developed Juche by making ideological breaks from Marxism-Leninism.
nodes.context_or_evidence: Kim Jong Il developed Juche in the 1980s and 1990s by making ideological breaks from Marxism-Leninism and increasing the importance of his father, Kim Il-sung.

nodes.content: Juche's guiding principles are independent stance, creative method, and giving precedence to ideological consciousness.
nodes.context_or_evidence: The guiding principles of Juche are independent stance, creative method, and giving precedence to ideological consciousness.

nodes.content: Emphasis on independence in Juche has led to Kim's Songun, or military first, politics.
nodes.context_or_evidence: Emphasis on independence in particular has given raise to Kim's Songun, or military first, politics.

nodes.content: COVID-19 vaccines are designed to provide acquired immunity against SARS-CoV-2, the virus causing COVID-19.
nodes.context_or_evidence: This is the fundamental purpose of COVID-19 vaccination.

nodes.content: Successful COVID-19 vaccine rollouts offer valuable lessons for future public health initiatives.
nodes.context_or_evidence: The COVID-19 pandemic response provides a case study for improving future strategies.

nodes.content: Integrating AI and machine learning into vaccine development can improve responses to emerging infectious diseases.
nodes.context_or_evidence: AI and machine learning can accelerate vaccine development and improve efficacy.

nodes.content: Leading COVID-19 vaccines, including Novavax, Johnson & Johnson, Pfizer/BioNTech, and Moderna, have shown reduced vaccine efficacy (VE) over time.
nodes.context_or_evidence: Studies indicate a decline in vaccine effectiveness against emerging variants.

nodes.content: Reduced vaccine efficacy is attributed to the emergence of new variants of SARS-CoV-2.
nodes.context_or_evidence: The evolution of the virus impacts the effectiveness of existing vaccines.

nodes.content: As of March 2021, thirteen vaccines had been approved for application, and over 90 vaccine candidates were under clinical trials.
nodes.context_or_evidence: This reflects the rapid pace of vaccine development during the COVID-19 pandemic.

nodes.content: Unprecedented financial investments, massive demand, previous scientific innovations, accelerated clinical development, and regulatory reviews accelerated COVID-19 vaccine development and approval.
nodes.context_or_evidence: Multiple factors contributed to the rapid development and deployment of COVID-19 vaccines.

nodes.content: As of 2025, at least 50 COVID-19 vaccines have received authorization in one or more countries.
nodes.context_or_evidence: This means the vaccines have been approved, licensed, or granted emergency use authorization by national regulatory authorities.

nodes.content: The United Kingdom's Medicines and Healthcare products Regulatory Agency (MHRA) gave temporary regulatory approval for the Pfizer–BioNTech vaccine on December 2, 2020.
nodes.context_or_evidence: The UK became the first country to approve the vaccine.

nodes.content: The FDA approved and authorized updated mRNA COVID-19 vaccines (2024-2025 formula) for emergency use.
nodes.context_or_evidence: The updated vaccines are designed to more closely target currently circulating variants to prevent COVID-19 and provide better protection.

nodes.content: The WHO provides a COVID-19 vaccine tracker and landscape.
nodes.context_or_evidence: The tracker includes information on key attributes of each vaccine candidate and allows users to search for COVID-19 vaccines through various criteria such as vaccine platform, schedule of vaccination, route of administration, developer, and trial phase.

nodes.content: The U.S. Government Bookstore offers Education & Libraries eBooks.
nodes.context_or_evidence: This is indicated by the title of the webpage and the category listing on the site.

nodes.content: mRNA vaccines offer advantages over traditional vaccines, including ease of design, speed and lower cost of production.
nodes.context_or_evidence: mRNA vaccines can be designed and produced more quickly and at a lower cost than traditional vaccines.

nodes.content: mRNA vaccines induce both cellular and humoral immunity.
nodes.context_or_evidence: mRNA vaccines stimulate both cellular and humoral immune responses, providing broad protection.

nodes.content: mRNA vaccines do not interact with the genomic DNA.
nodes.context_or_evidence: mRNA vaccines operate outside the nucleus and do not integrate into the host's genomic DNA, reducing the risk of insertional mutagenesis.

nodes.content: mRNA vaccine uptake/biodistribution and the innate immune response are critical for initiating adaptive immunity.
nodes.context_or_evidence: The process of mRNA vaccine entering cells, its distribution within the body, and the resulting innate immune response are essential steps in triggering the adaptive immune system.

nodes.content: COVID-19 mRNA vaccines have drawn increasing attention due to their application prospects and advantages, including a short development cycle.
nodes.context_or_evidence: The rapid development and deployment of mRNA vaccines during the COVID-19 pandemic highlighted their potential for quickly addressing emerging infectious diseases.

nodes.content: COVID-19 mRNA vaccines have drawn increasing attention due to their application prospects and advantages, including easy production.
nodes.context_or_evidence: mRNA vaccines are relatively easy to produce compared to traditional vaccines, allowing for rapid scaling of manufacturing capacity.

nodes.content: mRNA-based vaccines drew the interest of scientists because of its potent and versatile nature in mitigating the disease efficiently through increased translation.
nodes.context_or_evidence: mRNA vaccines can effectively reduce disease severity by promoting increased protein production (translation) within cells.

nodes.content: mRNA-based vaccines drew the interest of scientists because of its potent and versatile nature in mitigating the disease efficiently through robust modulation of the immune system.
nodes.context_or_evidence: mRNA vaccines can effectively reduce disease severity by triggering a strong and adaptable immune response.

nodes.title: COVID-19 mRNA vaccines: Platforms and current developments

nodes.content: The COVID-19 pandemic has highlighted the powerful clinical potential of mRNA vaccines.
nodes.context_or_evidence: The rapid development and deployment of mRNA vaccines during the COVID-19 pandemic demonstrated their effectiveness in preventing severe disease and reducing transmission.

nodes.content: Messenger RNA (mRNA) is a molecule that encodes a sequence that can be made into a protein.
nodes.context_or_evidence: mRNA carries the genetic instructions for protein synthesis, which is the basis for how mRNA vaccines work.
